About

My research focuses on nonlinear control design for inverter-fed electric drives under practical operating conditions — load disturbances, parameter variations, and speed transients. I have developed integral sliding mode controllers (ISMC, ASISMC, ASTISMC) for sensorless EV drivetrains and validated them experimentally on dSPACE (1202/1104) and OPAL-RT under standard drive cycles (NEDC, UDDS). This work connects control theory directly with power electronics hardware — a combination central to the next generation of converter-dominated energy systems.

On the hardware side, I led the development of a 3.7 kW motor drive system (industry-sponsored by Bharat Bijlee) — covering gate driver circuits, DC-DC auxiliary power supplies, shunt-based current sensing, and PCB layout. I also contributed to establishing the electric drive laboratory at IIT Jodhpur, including complete inverter, sensing, and motor drive setups built from scratch.

In parallel, I work as a Senior Motor Control Consultant at Reakar Electric India, handling controller validation, encoder signal processing, and model-based analysis of motor-converter systems under practical constraints.

I am currently in the final stage of my PhD and am actively seeking postdoctoral positions in power electronics, electric drives, microgrids, and energy management.

Key Expertise

Motor Control
FOC, DTC, robust control, Integral Sliding Mode Control (ISMC), sensorless speed estimation (MRAS)
Controllers
dSPACE, STM32, OPAL-RT (Basic), Arduino

EV drivetrains, industrial motor drives, microgrid optimisation

Experience

Sept 2025 – Present
Senior Motor Control Consultant Reakar Electric India
  • Controller verification and validation using real-time hardware data
  • Encoder calibration and signal filtering for precision position sensing
  • Motor modelling and model-based analysis of motor-converter systems
  • Handling practical implementation constraints: measurement noise and system nonlinearities
2019 – Present
PhD Research IIT Jodhpur  ·  Industry-sponsored by Bharat Bijlee

Development of 3.7 kW Motor Drive System

  • Designed and developed a complete 3.7 kW PMaSynRM motor drive system for electric vehicle and industrial applications
  • Designed hardware including PCB schematics, gate driver circuits, and auxiliary DC-DC power supply for power converters
  • Integrated shunt-based current sensing and precision thermal monitoring for robust operation under varying load and temperature conditions
  • Utilised the STM32F407 microcontroller for real-time motor control, enabling fast, deterministic execution of embedded control algorithms

Robust Controller Development for EV Drivetrain Applications

  • Developed sensorless control for inverter-fed EV drives to handle load variation, parameter uncertainty, and speed oscillations under practical conditions
  • Designed adaptive and robust control methods for low-cost EVs (E2Ws/E3Ws) to improve disturbance rejection under dynamic operation
  • Applied FOC and MTPA control to PMaSynRM drives for improved efficiency and handling of nonlinear motor behaviour
  • Developed a higher-order sliding mode controller (ASTISMC) to reduce torque ripple and improve speed tracking under varying disturbances
  • Validated all control strategies on real-time dSPACE/MicroLabBox (1202, 1104) under different load conditions and standard driving cycles (NEDC, UDDS)
2017 – 2019
M.Tech Research Aligarh Muslim University, Aligarh
  • Developed and experimentally implemented a supervisory energy management system for residential microgrids on a low-cost Arduino platform
  • Managed power flow between solar PV, battery storage, grid supply, and prioritised critical loads under constrained energy conditions

Experimental Setup & Laboratory

The hardware systems and experimental platforms below were developed and validated as part of doctoral research at IIT Jodhpur. Each setup was built in-house, from PCB-level design to full system integration and real-time controller deployment.

Hardware Setups

3.7 kW Motor Drive System In-house developed hardware including power module PCB, gate driver board, DC-DC auxiliary power supply, shunt-based current sensing, and thermal monitoring circuits — Bharat Bijlee sponsored project.
EV Lab Hardware Setup Complete test bench comprising dSPACE MicroLabBox controller, three-phase inverter, driver IC circuits, current/voltage sensors, DC generator load machine, and step-down transformer — used for real-time validation of sensorless controllers.
Microgrid Hardware Setup Experimental residential microgrid prototype comprising a DC source, inverter, DC-DC converter, battery storage, and a low-cost supervisory controller managing power flow between solar PV, grid, and prioritised loads.

Experimental Results

ASISMC — Performance under Periodic Oscillating Load (0.5 Hz, 1 Hz, 2 Hz) Controller tested under continuously varying load at multiple oscillation frequencies. Max deviation: 17.1 RPM · MAE: 2.48 RPM. Click to view all results.
ASISMC — Speed Tracking under Multi-Speed Reference & Step Load Sensorless speed control under alternating load torque (0.3–1.2 Nm) with speed steps between 300 RPM and 500 RPM. Max steady-state deviation: 14.1 RPM · MAE: 1.95 RPM.
ASISMC — Disturbance Rejection at Constant Speed (300 RPM) Step load torque variation (0.3–1 Nm) applied repeatedly at constant reference speed. Max deviation: 17.3 RPM · MAE: 2.11 RPM — showing effective disturbance rejection with rapid recovery.
ASISMC — Performance under Periodic Oscillating Load (0.5 Hz, 1 Hz, 2 Hz) Load torque at 0.5, 1, and 2 Hz oscillation frequencies at 1 Nm. Max deviation: 17.1 RPM · MAE: 2.48 RPM — confirming consistent rejection of dynamic disturbances.
ASISMC — Multi-Speed Reference under Periodic Oscillating Load (1 Hz) Speed reference stepped between 300 RPM and 500 RPM under periodic 1 Hz oscillating load (1–1.2 Nm). Max steady-state deviation: 29.2 RPM · MAE: 4.31 RPM.
